WHITNEY NURSING AND REHABILITATION CENTER

CCN: 676074 · WHITNEY, TX 76692 · Hill County

OverviewRatingsStaffingInspectionsDeficienciesPenaltiesFinancialOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data

Overview

Address
101 SAN MARCUS, WHITNEY, TX 76692
Phone
2546942233
Certified beds
88
Avg daily residents
62 (70% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
2005-11-01
Setting
Rural
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 2.93 4.49 2.51 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.30 0.79 0.26 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.96 1.00 0.83 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.66 2.71 1.43 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.27 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.01 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.62
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.29
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.25
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.59
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.16
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-09-19 7 6 1 44 1 44
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-07-28 7 2 7 270 1 270
Total weighted health score
100.5
lower is better

Deficiencies (16)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

12 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
4 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2026-01-30 2026-02-10
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-09-19 2024-10-09
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. E 2024-09-19 2024-10-07
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-09-19 2024-10-08
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. E 2024-09-19 2024-10-07
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-09-19 2024-09-20
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-09-19 2024-09-26
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2024-05-06 2024-05-08
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. J 2024-03-19 2024-03-28
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. J 2024-03-19 2024-03-21
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. J 2024-03-19 2024-03-28
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. J 2024-03-19 2024-03-28
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. E 2023-07-28 2023-08-23
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2023-07-28 2023-08-23
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2022-06-09 2022-07-10
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. D 2022-06-09 2022-06-22

  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
